title: Question: Docker Build
body:
---


Author Name: **Ryan Leonard**
Original Redmine Message: 2051 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi Wilson,

I'm working for Steve Hoover over at Redwood EDA.  We've been using Verilator on makerchip.com, an online IDE for hardware design, and loving it.

We're using Docker to run our testing, and created a Docker image of Verilator.  The image includes all dependencies needed to run Verilator, down to the operating system, and is currently 250mb.

I figured I'd give a shout, in case other users would appreciate this installation method.  The image is hosted at https://hub.docker.com/r/rweda/verilator/, along with usage information.

I'm hoping to add nightly builds in the near future, as well as building on Alpine Linux, which would reduce the image size by around 100mb.

While I was working on Alpine Linux builds, I ran into a compile issue where V3EmitC.cpp complains that ```format '%llx' expects argument of type 'long long unsigned int', but argument 3 has type 'uint64_t {aka long unsigned int}
```

It appears that some format called "PRIu64" would allow better portability.  Is this something you might consider?  http://stackoverflow.com/questions/8132399/how-to-printf-uint64-t

Full error:
```
g++ -I/usr/local/include -MMD -I. -I.. -I.. -I../../include -DYYDEBUG 	 -ggdb -DVL_DEBUG -MP  -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-char-subscripts -Wno-undefined-bool-conversion -fno-delete-null-pointer-checks -DDEFENV_SYSTEMC=\"\" -DDEFENV_SYSTEMC_ARCH=\"\" -DDEFENV_SYSTEMC_INCLUDE=\"\" -DDEFENV_SYSTEMC_LIBDIR=\"\" -DDEFENV_SYSTEMPERL=\"\" -DDEFENV_SYSTEMPERL_INCLUDE=\"\" -DDEFENV_VERILATOR_ROOT=\"/usr/local/share/verilator\" -c ../V3EmitC.cpp
../V3EmitC.cpp: In member function 'void EmitCImp::emitSavableImp(AstNodeModule*)':
../V3EmitC.cpp:1581:28: warning: format '%llx' expects argument of type 'long long unsigned int', but argument 3 has type 'uint64_t {aka long unsigned int}' [-Wformat=]
          hash.digestUInt64());
                             ^
At global scope:
cc1plus: warning: unrecognized command line option '-Wno-undefined-bool-conversion'

```

Thanks,
Ryan Leonard
code_blocks:
It appears that some format called "PRIu64" would allow better portability.  Is this something you might consider?  http://stackoverflow.com/questions/8132399/how-to-printf-uint64-t
